1	/**
2	 * Lesson Duplication Orchestrator
3	 *
4	 * Drives the duplication pipeline for a single LessonDuplications record:
5	 *  1. K2: Select up to 20 exercises from source lesson (via selectExercisesScaled)
6	 *  2. For each exercise, concurrently (max 3):
7	 *     a. Route to strategy (script or AI) → generates new exercise content
8	 *     b. Run structural validator on the generated content
9	 *     c. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script', run semantic validator
10	 *     d. On any failure, append a failure entry to the LessonDuplications record
11	 *  3. After all exercises settle: set status=succeeded (0 failures) or needs_review (>0 failures)
12	 *
13	 * Failures are streamed live — each failure is written to the DB immediately
14	 * via payload.update(), not accumulated in memory.
15	 */
16	
17	import type { Payload } from 'payload'
18	import type { Exercise } from '@/payload-types'
19	import type { ContentBlock } from '@/server/payload/collections/Exercises/schemas'
20	import type { DuplicationSubject } from '@/server/payload/collections/LessonDuplications'
21	import { logger } from '@/infra/utils/logger'
22	import { selectExercisesScaled } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/selectors'
23	import { getSourceExercisesForLesson } from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/source-exercises'
24	import {
25	  validateExerciseStructural,
26	  fillMissingFieldsWithPlaceholders,
27	  BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES,
28	  type StructuralFailure,
29	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/structural'
30	import {
31	  validateExerciseSemantic,
32	  SEMANTIC_FAILURE_CODE,
33	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/validators/semantic'
34	import {
35	  RouterStrategy,
36	  type VariationStrategy,
37	} from '@/server/services/lesson-duplication/strategies/router'
38	
39	// Concurrency of 1 = process exercises sequentially. Each exercise hits the
40	// LLM twice (creative + deterministic). Gemini's per-minute quota is easily
41	// exceeded at higher concurrency, which triggered "rate limit exceeded"
42	// failures on every exercise in early multi-exercise runs. The variation
43	// service has rate-limit backoff as a safety net, but serializing is the
44	// primary control. Bump this back up only after we've sized the quota AND
45	// replaced appendEntry's read-modify-write with an atomic $push.
46	// `as const` pins the literal type so the compile-time guard below catches
47	// any bump (current value preserved as literal `1`).
48	export const CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1 as const
49	
50	export const GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE = 'GENERATION_FAILED' as const
51	
52	export type DuplicationStrategy = 'script' | 'ai'
53	
54	/** Result of running the strategy for one exercise. */
55	export interface StrategyResult {
56	  exerciseId: string
57	  strategy: DuplicationStrategy
58	  blocks: ContentBlock[]
59	}
60	
61	/**
62	 * Generate a new exercise via the appropriate strategy.
63	 *
64	 * K3: ScriptVariationStrategy for purely-algebraic exercises at light level.
65	 * K4: AiVariationStrategy for all other cases (throws until AI is wired up).
66	 *
67	 * @param exercise    Source exercise to vary
68	 * @param level       Duplication level (none/light/medium/deep)
69	 * @param subject     Duplication subject
70	 * @param payload     Payload CMS instance
71	 * @param aiStrategy  Optional AI strategy override (for testing)
72	 * @returns           StrategyResult with generated blocks and strategy used
73	 */
74	export async function runStrategy(
75	  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
76	  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
77	  subject: DuplicationSubject,
78	  payload: Payload,
79	  aiStrategy?: VariationStrategy,
80	): Promise<StrategyResult> {
81	  const router = new RouterStrategy(payload, undefined, aiStrategy)
82	  const result = await router.apply(exercise as unknown as Exercise, level, subject)
83	
84	  const content = result.exercise.content as unknown as { blocks: ContentBlock[] }
85	  return {
86	    exerciseId: exercise.id,
87	    strategy: result.needsAiFallback ? 'ai' : 'script',
88	    blocks: content.blocks ?? [],
89	  }
90	}
91	
92	/** Suggested action for a failure, based on failure code. */
93	function suggestAction(code: string): 'skip' | 'regenerate' | 'keep' {
94	  switch (code) {
95	    case 'MISSING_QUESTION':
96	    case 'MISSING_HINT':
97	    case 'MISSING_SOLUTION':
98	    case 'MISSING_FULL_SOLUTION':
99	    case 'MISSING_CORRECT_OPTION':
100	    case 'MISSING_WRONG_OPTIONS':
101	    case 'INVALID_SVG':
102	    case 'PNG_FORBIDDEN':
103	    case 'TOO_MANY_SECTIONS':
104	    case 'INVALID_GEOMETRY_SPEC':
105	    case 'INVALID_AXIS_SPEC':
106	    case 'INVALID_GUIDED_EXPLANATION':
107	      return 'regenerate'
108	    case 'SEMANTIC_MISMATCH':
109	      return 'regenerate'
110	    case 'GENERATION_FAILED':
111	      return 'skip'
112	    default:
113	      return 'skip'
114	  }
115	}
116	
117	// Compile-time guard: this whole module assumes CONCURRENCY_LIMIT === 1.
118	// appendEntry below uses a non-atomic read-then-update on the failures/warnings
119	// arrays — at concurrency >1 two parallel appends would race and lose entries.
120	// If you raise CONCURRENCY_LIMIT, replace appendEntry with a Mongo $push update
121	// before deleting this assert.
122	type _AssertConcurrencyOne = typeof CONCURRENCY_LIMIT extends 1 ? true : never
123	const _concurrencyAssert: _AssertConcurrencyOne = true
124	void _concurrencyAssert
125	
126	/**
127	 * Append a single failure/warning entry to the LessonDuplications record.
128	 *
129	 * The bucket name is chosen by the caller:
130	 *  - 'failures' = blocking; the exercise was dropped from the output lesson.
131	 *  - 'warnings' = non-blocking; the exercise was kept with TODO placeholders
132	 *    for the missing field. Admin polishes via the review screen.
133	 */
134	async function appendEntry(
135	  bucket: 'failures' | 'warnings',
136	  payload: Payload,
137	  duplicationId: string,
138	  exerciseRef: string,
139	  sectionIndex: number,
140	  code: string,
141	  message: string,
142	): Promise<void> {
143	  const action = suggestAction(code)
144	  try {
145	    const current = await payload.findByID({
146	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
147	      id: duplicationId,
148	      depth: 0,
149	      overrideAccess: true,
150	    })
151	    await payload.update({
152	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
153	      id: duplicationId,
154	      data: {
155	        [bucket]: [
156	          // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
157	          ...(((current as any)[bucket] as any[]) ?? []),
158	          {
159	            exerciseRef,
160	            sectionIndex,
161	            code,
162	            message,
163	            suggestedAction: action,
164	            resolved: false,
165	          },
166	        ],
167	      } as never,
168	      overrideAccess: true,
169	    })
170	  } catch (err) {
171	    logger.error(
172	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef, code, bucket },
173	      `Failed to append ${bucket} entry to LessonDuplications`,
174	    )
175	    // Don't rethrow — streaming failure should not abort the run
176	  }
177	}
178	
179	/** Append a blocking failure (exercise dropped from output lesson). */
180	async function appendFailure(
181	  payload: Payload,
182	  duplicationId: string,
183	  exerciseRef: string,
184	  sectionIndex: number,
185	  code: string,
186	  message: string,
187	): Promise<void> {
188	  return appendEntry('failures', payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, sectionIndex, code, message)
189	}
190	
191	/** Append a non-blocking warning (exercise kept with placeholder). */
192	async function appendWarning(
193	  payload: Payload,
194	  duplicationId: string,
195	  exerciseRef: string,
196	  sectionIndex: number,
197	  code: string,
198	  message: string,
199	): Promise<void> {
200	  return appendEntry('warnings', payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, sectionIndex, code, message)
201	}
202	
203	/**
204	 * Append a successful output-exercise mapping to the LessonDuplications
205	 * record. Used to stream progress so a Vercel function timeout doesn't lose
206	 * work that's already in the DB. Read-modify-write on the record's
207	 * `outputExercises` array — safe under CONCURRENCY_LIMIT = 1.
208	 */
209	async function appendOutputExercise(
210	  payload: Payload,
211	  duplicationId: string,
212	  mapping: OutputExerciseMapping,
213	): Promise<void> {
214	  try {
215	    const current = await payload.findByID({
216	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
217	      id: duplicationId,
218	      depth: 0,
219	      overrideAccess: true,
220	    })
221	    const existing =
222	      (current as unknown as { outputExercises?: OutputExerciseMapping[] }).outputExercises ?? []
223	    await payload.update({
224	      collection: 'lesson-duplications',
225	      id: duplicationId,
226	      data: { outputExercises: [...existing, mapping] } as never,
227	      overrideAccess: true,
228	    })
229	  } catch (err) {
230	    logger.error(
231	      { err, duplicationId, exerciseRef: mapping.sourceExerciseId },
232	      'Failed to append output exercise mapping',
233	    )
234	  }
235	}
236	
237	/** Shape of an exercise from the exercises collection. */
238	type ExerciseDoc = {
239	  id: string
240	  content?: { blocks?: ContentBlock[] }
241	}
242	
243	/** Mapping entry from source exercise to generated output exercise. */
244	interface OutputExerciseMapping {
245	  sourceExerciseId: string
246	  outputExerciseId: string
247	  strategy: DuplicationStrategy
248	}
249	
250	/**
251	 * Create the output lesson (draft) by deep-copying every source-lesson field.
252	 *
253	 * Earlier versions only copied title/slug/status/chapter/course/tenant/locale,
254	 * which dropped fields like accessType, visibleRenderers, blocks, contentStatus,
255	 * and any custom flags the source lesson had. This caused variation outputs to
256	 * be thinner than what the `none` endpoint produces. We now mirror the
257	 * deep-clone behaviour of the `none` path so both routes produce equivalent
258	 * lessons modulo the title suffix and a forced `draft` status.
259	 */
260	async function createOutputLesson(
261	  payload: Payload,
262	  sourceLessonId: string,
263	  level: string,
264	): Promise<string> {
265	  const source = await payload.findByID({
266	    collection: 'lessons',
267	    id: sourceLessonId,
268	    depth: 0,
269	    overrideAccess: true,
270	  })
271	  const sourceData = source as unknown as Record<string, unknown>
272	  // Drop Payload-managed + lineage fields; the rest carries over.
273	  //   slug:           regenerated by formatSlugAsync from the new title.
274	  //   blocks:         references source exercise IDs; rebuilt by the exercise
275	  //                   auto-add hook as new variation exercises are saved.
276	  //   translatedFrom: this is a fresh variation, not a translation copy.
277	  //   createdBy:      set to the duplicating admin via createdByField hook.
278	  const {
279	    id: _id,
280	    createdAt: _c,
281	    updatedAt: _u,
282	    slug: _ignoreSlug,
283	    blocks: _ignoreBlocks,
284	    translatedFrom: _ignoreTranslatedFrom,
285	    createdBy: _ignoreCreatedBy,
286	    ...rest
287	  } = sourceData as Record<string, unknown> & {
288	    id?: unknown
289	    createdAt?: unknown
290	    updatedAt?: unknown
291	    slug?: unknown
292	    blocks?: unknown
293	    translatedFrom?: unknown
294	    createdBy?: unknown
295	  }
296	  void _id
297	  void _c
298	  void _u
299	  void _ignoreSlug
300	  void _ignoreBlocks
301	  void _ignoreTranslatedFrom
302	  void _ignoreCreatedBy
303	  const base = (rest.title as string) ?? 'Lesson'
304	  const newLesson = await payload.create({
305	    collection: 'lessons',
306	    data: {
307	      ...rest,
308	      title: `${base} - Variation (${level})`,
309	      status: 'draft', // never publish a duplicate by default
310	    } as never,
311	    overrideAccess: true,
312	  })
313	  return newLesson.id
314	}
315	
316	/**
317	 * Create a draft exercise in the output lesson with the generated blocks.
318	 * Returns the mapping entry for tracking.
319	 */
320	async function createOutputExercise(
321	  payload: Payload,
322	  result: StrategyResult,
323	  outputLessonId: string,
324	): Promise<OutputExerciseMapping> {
325	  const ex = await payload.create({
326	    collection: 'exercises',
327	    data: {
328	      title: `Variation of ${result.exerciseId}`,
329	      lesson: outputLessonId,
330	      content: { blocks: result.blocks },
331	      status: 'draft',
332	    } as never,
333	    overrideAccess: true,
334	  })
335	  return {
336	    sourceExerciseId: result.exerciseId,
337	    outputExerciseId: ex.id,
338	    strategy: result.strategy,
339	  }
340	}
341	
342	/**
343	 * Process a single exercise through the duplication pipeline.
344	 *
345	 * Steps:
346	 *  1. Run strategy → get new exercise content
347	 *  2. Structural validation → collect failures
348	 *  3. If structural passes AND strategy != 'script' → semantic validation
349	 *  4. On any failure: stream failure to DB, return null
350	 *  5. On all pass: return StrategyResult for the orchestrator to use
351	 */
352	async function processExercise(
353	  exercise: ExerciseDoc,
354	  duplicationId: string,
355	  level: 'none' | 'light' | 'medium' | 'deep',
356	  subject: DuplicationSubject,
357	  payload: Payload,
358	  aiStrategy?: VariationStrategy,
359	): Promise<StrategyResult | null> {
360	  const exerciseRef = exercise.id
361	
362	  // Step 1: Run strategy
363	  let strategyResult: StrategyResult
364	  try {
365	    strategyResult = await runStrategy(exercise, level, subject, payload, aiStrategy)
366	  } catch (err) {
367	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown strategy error'
368	    logger.error({ exerciseRef, level, err }, 'Strategy generation failed')
369	    await appendFailure(payload, duplicationId, exerciseRef, 0, GENERATION_FAILURE_CODE, message)
370	    return null
371	  }
372	
373	  // Step 2: Structural validation. Split failures into blocking (drop the
374	  // exercise — renderer would crash) and warnings (admin fills from review
375	  // screen). Warning-only exercises still ship, with TODO placeholders filled
376	  // in for missing hint/solution/fullSolution so the lesson stays renderable.
377	  const structuralFailures: StructuralFailure[] = validateExerciseStructural(strategyResult.blocks)
378	  const blockingFailures = structuralFailures.filter((f) => BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES.has(f.code))
379	  const warningFailures = structuralFailures.filter((f) => !BLOCKING_FAILURE_CODES.has(f.code))
380	
381	  // Record blocking failures and non-blocking warnings into separate buckets
382	  // so the review UI can show them under different headings.
383	  for (const failure of blockingFailures) {
384	    await appendFailure(
385	      payload,
386	      duplicationId,
387	      exerciseRef,
388	      failure.blockIndex ?? 0,
389	      failure.code,
390	      failure.message,
391	    )
392	  }
393	  for (const warning of warningFailures) {
394	    await appendWarning(
395	      payload,
396	      duplicationId,
397	      exerciseRef,
398	      warning.blockIndex ?? 0,
399	      warning.code,
400	      warning.message,
401	    )
402	  }
403	
404	  if (blockingFailures.length > 0) {
405	    return null
406	  }
407	
408	  // Warning-only path: fill placeholders so the exercise renders. Warnings are
409	  // already recorded so the admin can find the TODOs in the review screen.
410	  if (warningFailures.length > 0) {
411	    strategyResult = {
412	      ...strategyResult,
413	      blocks: fillMissingFieldsWithPlaceholders(strategyResult.blocks),
414	    }
415	  }
416	
417	  // Step 3: Semantic validation. Skipped for:
418	  //  - level=none (deep clone, no AI variation to judge)
419	  //  - script strategy (deterministic, no hallucination risk)
420	  //  - warning-only path (we just inserted "_TODO: hint not provided by AI_"
421	  //    placeholders; a semantic reviewer would flag those as nonsensical and
422	  //    demote an exercise that should ship with a polish-later flag into a
423	  //    hard failure)
424	  if (strategyResult.strategy !== 'script' && level !== 'none' && warningFailures.length === 0) {
425	    const semanticResult = await validateExerciseSemantic(
426	      strategyResult.blocks,
427	      level,
428	      strategyResult.strategy,
429	      payload,
430	    )
431	    if (!semanticResult.ok) {
432	      await appendFailure(
433	        payload,
434	        duplicationId,
435	        exerciseRef,
436	        0,
437	        SEMANTIC_FAILURE_CODE,
438	        `Semantic mismatch: ${semanticResult.reasons.join('; ')}`,
439	      )
440	      return null
441	    }
442	  }
443	
444	  return strategyResult
445	}

447	/**
448	 * Reserve at least this much wall time (ms) at the end of a tick so the
449	 * orchestrator can write its final state to the DB before the platform
450	 * (Vercel) yanks the function. 30s covers a slow Mongo round-trip plus
451	 * Vercel's teardown grace.
452	 */

458	 * exercise can fit before the deadline. Conservative — if the run is
459	 * faster, we just process more per tick.
